data.title: Colombia finance minister says 2025 growth estimate will likely be increased to 2.8%
data.description:
Colombia finance minister German Avila said on Friday that the economic growth estimate for the country this year will "probably" be increased to 2.8% from 2.6%.

The uptick would put the government projection in line with that of the central bank, which on Monday raised its own estimate.

President Gustavo Petro has repeatedly pressured the central bank board to lower the benchmark interest rate to boost growth, but the seven members of the body have held borrowing costs steady at 9.5% for two consecutive meetings on inflation and external concerns.

Avila, speaking at a pension funds conference in Cartagena, said the government was looking to cut costs, improve tax take and better administer debt and added that it will likely be necessary to find new markets for debt for the country.

"Surely a search for new markets will be necessary, new possibilities for restructuring of the most costly debt that the country currently has," Avila said.

data.title: Starbucks Same Store Sales May Improve, Oppenheimer Says
data.description:
Starbucks' same store sales may improve in the quarters ahead albeit with "less powerful upside than required to turn bullish," Oppenheimer said in a Thursday note.

Oppenheimer analysts said they are staying on the sidelines on the stock even after recent pullback.

"We lack visibility into catalysts for multiple expansion to 30x+ and will stick with our Perform rating until we can identify

a more attractive fundamental setup," they said.

The analysts forecast fiscal 2026 EPS estimate of $3.52, below market consensus of $3.64.

"We're skeptical of the margin expansion built-in," they said.

data.title: Belgrade bourse BELEX15 rises on Aerodrom gains
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), April 4 (SeeNews) - The blue-chip index of the Belgrade Stock Exchange, the BELEX15, rose by 0.41% on Friday to close at 1,150.77 points, lifted by gains in Aerodrom Nikola Tesla [BEL:AERO], bourse data showed.

Aerodrom Nikola Tesla, the only blue-chip gainer on Friday, advanced by 3.55%, closing at 2,071 dinars in a trade of 1,405 shares.

Cookware maker Metalac [BEL:MTLC] led the blue-chip decliners, by falling by 1.02% to close at 1,950 dinars as 92 shares traded. Insurer Dunav Osiguranje [BEL:DNOS] lost 1%, closing at 1,490 dinars as 962 shares traded.

Industrial gases producer Messer Tehnogas [BEL:TGAS], which generated the single largest turnover of 4.56 million dinars, shed 0.01% to close at 33,998 dinars as 136 shares traded.

Aluminium rolling mill Impol Seval [BEL: IMPL] closed unchanged at 7,600 dinars as four shares traded.

No other blue-chip companies traded on Friday.

The broader BELEXline, a free-float market capitalisation-weighted index, closed 0.16% higher at 2,506.20 points.

Total turnover on the Belgrade bourse rose to 23.7 million dinars ($222,429/ 202,306 euro) from 10.5 million dinars on Thursday. Trading in government securities contributed some 11 million dinars of the total on Friday, compared to 5.9 million dinars on Thursday.

(1 euro = 117.149 dinars)

data.title: Massive Bitcoin, Altcoin Volatility as Trump’s Trade War Triggers Retaliation: This Week’s Crypto Recap
data.description:
It was another big week in the cryptocurrency markets, filled with notable developments and big price moves propelled by US President Donald Trump’s global political actions.

More precisely, his Trade War that escalated this week. In what he called ‘Liberation Day,’ the POTUS announced massive tariffs against numerous countries, including some of the country’s biggest partners. That was on April 2 and they should be incorporated on April 5.

Naturally, many countries and regions decided to fight back by announcing tariffs of their own. The latest to do so was China, which earlier today imposed a 34% retaliatory tariff on all goods imported from the United States.

These developments led to substantial volatility in all financial markets, including crypto. Bitcoin began the business week with a price slide to $81,200 but quickly started to regain traction and skyrocketed to just over $88,000 on Wednesday amidreportsthat Trump will remove Elon Musk from his inner circle.

However, that was just hours ahead of the tariff announcements on ‘Liberation Day’ and BTC quickly reversed its trajectory. In a matter of just a few hours, the cryptocurrency plunged by several grand and fell to $82,300. Its rebound was unsuccessful and bitcoin dumped to $81,200 on Thursday.

Another failed recovery attempt transpired today when BTC neared $85,000, but then the Chinese retaliation tariffs came on. Bitcoin reacted with an immediate plunge to $81,600. It now struggles below $83,000, being 2.5% down weekly.

More substantial weekly losses come from several altcoins, led by TON (-14%), LINK (-11%), SIU (-12.5%), and SOL (-10%). ETH, ADA, BNB, and DOGE are also deep in the red.Market Data

Market Cap: $2.748T | 24H Vol: $112B | BTC Dominance: 59.8%

BTC: $82,720 (-4.5%) | ETH: $1,788 (-5.3%) | XRP: $2.1 (-4.2%)This Week’s Crypto Headlines You Can’t Miss

Arthur Hayes Predicts Money Printing Boom, Bullish for Bitcoin. With everyone focused on the Trade War, multiple experts have rushed to offer their opinion on how these uncertain times can impact BTC and the financial markets. Arthur Hayesoutlinedhis positive prediction, indicating that bitcoin has to maintain this level by US tax day (April 15) to remain in a bull cycle structure.

Here’s Why Ethereum (ETH) Continues to Bleed, According to CryptoQuant. Ethereum continues to underperform, with its price tanking below $1,800 earlier this week. CryptoQuant named a few reasons behind ETH’s struggles, including the diminishing network activity.

Are Trump’s Tariffs Impacting Cryptocurrencies as Expected? Santiment Weighs In. The tariffs implemented by Trump and replicated by numerous other countries in response have an undeniable and immediate impact on BTC and the crypto market. However, what are the long-term effects? You can find out here.

Stablecoin Issuer Circle Files for IPO After Big Revenue Report. The more positive regulatory landscape in the United States allowed Circle, the company behind the second-largest stablecoin, to file for an Initial Public Offering (IPO). This came after a strong revenue report from the firm for Q1, 2025.

Metaplanet Increases Bitcoin Holdings to 4,046 BTC with Latest Acquisition. Metaplanet, following the example by Michael Saylor’s Strategy, seems unfazed by the global economic uncertainty and continues to purchase BTC frequently. Saylor’s company did the same this week.

Bitcoin, Ether Post Worst First Quarter Performance in 7 Years. Despite the big expectations for February and March, Q1 of this year turned out to be the worst for the two largest cryptocurrencies by market cap. BTC dropped by just shy of 12%, while ETH’s price tumbled by more than 45%.Charts

This week, we have a chart analysis of Ethereum, Ripple, Cardano, Shiba Inu, and Solana – click here for the complete price analysis.

data.title: PayPal Adds Chainlink, Solana to Cryptocurrency Offerings on Platform
data.description:
PayPal said Friday that two more cryptocurrencies, Chainlink and Solana, are available for PayPal and Venmo users to buy, hold, sell, and transfer via their digital wallets.

The company said PayPal and Venmo users will begin seeing the two tokens available for purchase over the next couple of weeks.

PayPal shares were falling past 5% in recent trading amid a broadly weaker market.

data.title: Nickel is down by 5%
data.description: Nickel decreased 5% to 14930 USD/T

data.title: Retail investors buy stocks at largest level in 10 years, JPMorgan says
data.description:
Retail investors bought $4.7 billion in stocks on Thursday, the highest level over the past decade, JPMorgan said in a note on Friday.

The historic "buy-to-dip" move by retail investors included names such as Nvidia , Amazon and S&P exchange-traded funds, but they sold Tesla .

Small-cap technology was one of individuals' favorite sector picks amid the meltdown, while institutional investors have increased their bets against the sector.

The S&P 500 plunged 4.9% on Thursday, its biggest one-day percentage loss since June 2020. The fall was triggered by U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of new import tariffs, which sparked fears of a global recession.

Retail investors' buying spree on Thursday was in contrast to their behavior during the COVID-inspired selloff in March 2020, JPMorgan said.

JPMorgan said that retail performance on Thursday was in line with the losses posted by S&P 500 index on the day, while individuals' stock portfolios are down 12.9% year-to-date on average, below the index.

data.title: FairWind successfully places EUR 75 million new senior secured sustainability-linked bonds
data.description:
Force BidCo A/S

Force BidCo A/S ("FairWind" or the "Company") has successfully placed new senior secured sustainability-linked floating rate bonds (ISIN SE0024321491) in the amount of EUR 75 million under a framework of EUR 150 million (the "Bonds" or the "Bond Issue"). The Bonds will have a 4-year tenor and carry a floating rate coupon of 3-months EURIBOR plus 7.5 per cent. per annum.

The proceeds from the Bond Issue will be used to fully redeem the Company’s outstanding bonds 2021/2026 with ISIN SE0016275820 (the “Existing Bonds”), repay other existing debt within the Group, pay transaction costs, as well as for general corporate purposes of the Company (including investments and future acquisitions).

Stewart Mitchell, CEO at FairWind, said: “It is a very difficult market at the moment so we are extremely pleased to have achieved this refinance. We have a robust growth strategy, securing these Bonds will support our expansion into new markets and drive our mergers and acquisitions ambitions.”

In accordance with previously announced information in the press release published on 26 March 2025, the Existing Bonds will be fully redeemed on 23 April 2025 at a redemption price of 100.9375 per cent. of their nominal amount, together with accrued but unpaid interest up to (and including) the redemption date. The redemption remains conditional upon the successful issuance of the Bonds on or before the record date for the redemption, 14 April 2025, unless such condition is waived by the Company at its sole discretion. Settlement for the Bond Issue is expected to occur on 10 April 2025. Once settlement has occurred, the Company will issue a further press release confirming fulfilment of the condition and provide final details regarding the redemption process.

The Company intends to apply for admission to trading of the Bonds on the sustainable bond list of Nasdaq Stockholm and the Nasdaq Transfer Market.

ABG Sundal Collier AB and Arctic Securities AS, filial Sverige act as joint bookrunners and managers and Gernandt & Danielsson Advokatbyrå KB acts as legal counsel to the joint bookrunners and Advokatfirman Cederquist KB acts as legal counsel to the Company in connection with the Bond Issue.

For further information:

Kathryn MacKenzie, Head of Marketing and Communications

kmackenzie@fairwind.com | +44 7549 690 023

About FairWind

FairWind A/S is a leading global provider of one-stop solutions for the installation and service of onshore and offshore wind turbines throughout the world. FairWind has worked in more than 40 countries and cooperates with over 2,200 technicians to manage customers’ projects globally.

You can read more about FairWind on www.fairwind.com

https://news.cision.com/force-bidco-a-s/r/fairwind-successfully-places-eur-75-million-new-senior-secured-sustainability-linked-bonds%2Cc4131372

Project Zephyr- Press release (close of books) (4 April 2025)(6786354.3)

Force Bidco FairWind - non-report

data.title: Duke Energy Restores Power for Over 98% of Customers in Indiana Following Outages
data.description:
Duke Energy said Friday it has restored power as of 9 am ET for over 98% of its customers affected by power outages in Indiana caused by Wednesday's storm.

As of Friday morning, storm-related power outages under Duke Energy's service territory have declined to about 1,000 from almost 97,000, and the company said its restoration efforts continue.

data.title: Restaurant Brands Intl Is Maintained at Buy by Guggenheim
data.description: (14:40 GMT) Restaurant Brands Intl Price Target Raised to $77.00/Share From $71.00 by Guggenheim

data.title: Wheat Hits 1-Month Low
data.description:
Wheat futures dropped toward $5.20 per bushel, their lowest level since March 4, after China announced a 34% retaliatory tariff on U.S. goods.

The move came in response to President Donald Trump’s latest trade measures, which included a 10% baseline tariff on all U.S. imports and additional levies on key trading partners, including a 34% duty on Chinese products.

China had already imposed tariffs on $21 billion worth of U.S. agricultural goods in response to earlier U.S. trade actions.

On the supply side, the USDA’s March Prospective Plantings report projected a 2% decline in U.S. wheat acreage this year, falling to 45.35 million acres—slightly below market expectations of 46.50 million.

If realized, this would mark the second-lowest all-wheat planted area since 1919.

Meanwhile, wheat stocks as of March 1 totaled 1.24 billion bushels, a 14% increase from last year and slightly above the consensus estimate of 1.22 billion.

data.title: Ageas Re adds Scor’s Monfared for engineering entry
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Ageas Re, the reinsurance arm of Belgian insurance group Ageas, has launched an engineering practice led by Shahrokh Shahpoori Monfared.

Monfared has joined Ageas Re after almost five years at French reinsurer Scor, where he served as treaty underwriting manager with responsibility for engineering clients across the UK, Switzerland, Central and Eastern Europe, and Middle East and Africa.

Prior to Scor, he held senior engineering underwriting roles at Axis Re.

Monfared will join Ageas Re's Zurich office on May 1.

Ageas Re hired long-serving PartnerRe underwriter Jeremy Lilburn in March as co-head of its newly launched credit and bond offering.

Parent company Ageas in February reported that net operating profit at its reinsurance unit jumped by 62% in 2024, which helped drive a group-level earnings and profit beat.

data.title: BK Technologies Debuts RelayONE Portable Repeater Kit, Receives Purchase Order From Larimer County
data.description:
BK Technologies said Friday it launched RelayONE, a portable repeater kit that is designed to promote interoperability between various types of public safety and military radios.

The company also said it received a purchase order for RelayONE kits from the Larimer County Sheriff's Office in Fort Collins, Colorado, for use by the county's search and rescue operations.

Financial terms of the order were not disclosed.

Shares of the company were down 5.9% in recent trading.

Glencore is one of the world's largest global diversified natural resource companies and a major producer and marketer of more than 60 commodities that advance everyday life. Through a network of assets, customers and suppliers that spans the globe, we produce, process, recycle, source, market and distribute the commodities that support decarbonisation while meeting the energy needs of today.

data.title: InsurTech NY panel: Communication and prevention key to managing climate risks
data.description:
(The Insurer) - The insurance industry must prioritise prevention and clear communication to mitigate risks associated with the rising frequency and severity of natural disasters, according to panellists at the InsurTech NY conference.

Speaking on a panel titled Preparing for the Next Natural Disaster, Adam Troyer, global head of analytics at Lockton Re, said that insurers are struggling to price and manage risks that are evolving faster than before.

“In the last 10 years, we have seen exposure growth and the impacts of climate change,” Troyer said on Thursday. “We’re now dealing with core and secondary perils that we’ve known about for a long time, but we’re seeing them with greater frequency and severity.”

Given this shift, he posed the question: “How do we, as an industry and as carriers, price, mitigate, manage and transfer risk that we thought we understood, but maybe don’t know as well anymore?”

Anna Holzapfel, innovation manager at Grange Insurance, pointed to outdated data as a big challenge in predicting risks.

“One of the key reasons predictive models have gaps is that we’re training them on historical data,” she said.

As a result, traditional models often fail to account for emerging risks such as the intensifying impact of hurricanes and shifting development patterns.

“Neighbourhoods that weren’t in flood zones 20 years ago are now in flood zones. Urban expansion, drainage differences and climate change are rewriting the rulebook on predictive analytics and how our models perform across the industry,” she said.

BRINGING PREVENTION BACK INTO FOCUS

Patrick Blandford, founder and CEO of Green Shield Risk Solutions, underlined the importance of returning to a prevention-focused mindset in risk management.

“When I started in the insurance industry, the big conversation was around predicting, preventing and transferring risk,” he said. “I think as an industry, we’ve lost sight of the ‘prevent’ part, and that’s at the heart of what our analytics should be used for.”

He explained that while many models focus on community-wide hazard risks, Green Shield takes a more granular approach.

“We look at risk at the structure and parcel level – what can be improved? And we also use propagation modelling, using 20 years of historical weather data to run simulations fundamentally at the 99th percentile event,” he said.

“This helps us understand how wildfires spread and what can be done to make communities and properties more defensible.”

Isaac Espinoza, CEO of Kettle, highlighted the importance of managing aggregate exposure to ensure that carriers are not overly concentrated in high-risk areas.

“You have to think about all the different types of events that could happen and how multiple risks could potentially be impacted by the same event,” Espinoza said.

He referenced the rare yet devastating wildfires January, cautioning insurers to avoid excessive exposure that could lead to unsustainable losses.

EDUCATING POLICYHOLDERS AND LEVERAGING TECHNOLOGY

Grange's Holzapfel also commented on the importance of educating homeowners and communities about proactive mitigation measures.

“We invest heavily in making sure policyholders have the right tools for prevention,” she said, highlighting social media as an effective channel for communication.

“We use our strong social media brand to reach policyholders and independent agents in the way they prefer to be communicated with – whether it’s a quick post about dropping temperatures and frozen pipe prevention, or more casual reminders about mitigation steps,” she said.

“We really try to equip not just our independent agents but (also) our policyholders with preventive actions to reduce the risk and reduce high claim volume for their own policies.”

Lockton Re's Troyer added that technology has significantly improved communication before and after disasters.

“With today’s technology, we can now communicate with agents and policyholders before an event, making sure that they’re prepared,” he said.

Post disaster, Troyer explained that insurers can use satellite imagery and other tools to assess damage and provide quick updates.

“We can now get very precise impact estimates and communicate them to policyholders, probably before they can even get in to see their homes themselves,” Troyer said.

“This not only improves the customer experience during difficult times, but it also makes the whole value chain more efficient and results in a better experience for everybody.”

data.title: Hiring Defied Expectations in March, With 228,000 New Jobs
data.description:
By Harriet Torry

Employers added jobs in March at a much stronger pace than expected, a sign that the labor market remained strong despite economic uncertainty and market turbulence.

The U.S. added 228,000 jobs in March, the Labor Department reported Friday, well above the gain of 140,000 jobs economists polled by The Wall Street Journal had expected to see.

That was more than the 117,000 jobs added in February.

The report offers relief about the labor market given recent concerns about growth, said Sarah House, senior economist for Wells Fargo. Still, she added that "while hiring was holding up in March, there's a lot of new challenges ahead."

The unemployment rate, which is based on a separate survey from the jobs figures, ticked up to 4.2%.

The Department of Government Efficiency's federal-government layoffs were a modest drag on payrolls. Federal government employment declined by just 4,000 in March, after dropping 11,000 in February.

The stronger-than-expected payrolls number follows a tumultuous couple of days for financial markets, which have been roiled by the Trump administration's tariff announcements. Employees who were on paid leave or receiving ongoing severance were counted as employed.

Healthcare, social assistance and the transportation sector added jobs.

Stock futures were down sharply before the report, pushed lower by continued concerns about the tariff plans announced late Wednesday. They remained in the red after the report was released.

Markets have been jolted this year by the unfolding impact of the Trump administration's trade policy and government layoffs.

Late Wednesday, the Trump administration announced sweeping new tariffs on countries around the world. The scope and depth of President Trump's plans shocked investors and businesses.

U.S. stocks plunged on Thursday, and bonds rallied. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite all logged their worst percentage declines since 2020.

American households and businesses are becoming more jittery about the economy's future due to uncertainty about trade policy and rising costs, according to recent surveys.

Regular Americans are squaring up to the prospect of continued high borrowing costs, persistent inflation and tariffs on a wide array of imported goods. Measures of consumer sentiment from the Conference Board and the University of Michigan tumbled again in March. Two-thirds of consumers in the latest Michigan survey said they expect unemployment to rise in the year ahead, the highest reading since 2009.

"Three to four months ago, everyone was pretty upbeat; we thought Trump was going to turbocharge the economy with deregulation and tax cuts — and instead we've got austerity, trade protectionism and immigration controls," said James Knightley, chief international economist at ING Financial Markets.

Some of that hesitation is translating into weak spending. In February, inflation-adjusted consumer spending edged up just 0.1% from the prior month, according to the Commerce Department, though that was better than January when it declined.

In recent weeks, companies including shipping giant FedEx, retailer Kohl's and sportswear brand Nike lowered their outlook for sales. Meanwhile, several big airlines cut near-term financial forecasts.

The Federal Reserve cut its growth projections for this year at its most recent policy meeting, while raising its forecasts for the unemployment rate and inflation. The central bank held steady its benchmark federal-funds rate at around 4.3%; its next scheduled meeting is in a month's time, May 6-7.

Write to Harriet Torry at harriet.torry@wsj.com

data.title: Citigroup Is Maintained at Buy by Truist Securities
Canada Labor-Market Headwinds to Intensify But BOC Likely Stands Pat This MonthMarket Talk
data.description: Canada's employment report for March provides some of the first pieces of tangible evidence about how President Trump's trade policy is starting to squeeze the country's economy, says TD Securities. Yet, the firm says the 33,000 job loss in the month and the increase in the unemployment rate won't be enough to push the Bank of Canada toward another rate cut. The firm notes that the economy has added, on a per-month average, 30,000 jobs since October, and adds that Statistics Canada's labor-force survey is a "notorious volatile data point." TD Securities does expect labor-market headwinds to intensify, and that will lead to an additional three rate cuts later this year. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

data.title: Nasdaq heads toward bear market as trade war worries grow
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes down: S&P 500 3.3%, Nasdaq 3.7%, Dow 3%
  • March payrolls beat expectations
  • Fed chair Jerome Powell's speech at 11:25 am ET

Wall Street fell sharply for a second straight session on Friday, pushing the Nasdaq toward a bear market , after China imposed fresh tariffs on all U.S. goods in response to the Trump administration's sweeping levies , escalating a global trade war.

The Nasdaq Composite fell 3.69% to 15,940.08 by 09:41 a.m. ET, shedding 20% from its all-time closing high touched in December. If the index closes below that mark, it would confirm a bear market.

The tariff war has sent shockwaves through global financial markets and raised the fears of an economic downturn, with investment bank JP Morgan forecasting a 60% chance of the global economy entering recession by year-end, up from 40% previously.

China's finance ministry said on Friday it would impose additional tariffs of 34% on all U.S. goods from April 10 after U.S. President Donald Trump raised tariff barriers to their highest level in more than a century this week.

U.S.-listings of Chinese companies dived, with JD.com and Alibaba shedding nearly 8.5% each and Baidu falling 7.6%.

Companies with exposure to China also fell across the board, with mega-caps such as Apple falling 4.7%, Nvidia losing 3.4% and Amazon.com slumping 6%.

"We're beginning to see the inevitable retaliation from the global trade partners of the United States. The risk is that this tips a recession scare into a full-blown recession," said Ben Laidler, head of equity strategy at Bradesco BBI.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 2.95% to 39,348.79, dropping 10% from its record close and on course to confirm a correction . The S&P 500 dropped 3.3% to 5,216.99.

The CBOE Volatility index , known as Wall Street's fear gauge, hit its highest level since August 2024 at 37.66 points.

Wall Street's main indexes posted their biggest single-day percentage declines in years on Thursday after Trump imposed a 10% tariff on most imports into the United States and much higher levies on dozens of other countries.

Investors have shunned riskier assets including stocks and commodities in recent weeks on bets that the tariffs will spark an economic slowdown, prompting them to seek safer assets such as government bonds and gold.

U.S. bank stocks dropped further on Friday, with the sector under pressure globally, as investors anticipated more interest rate cuts from central banks and a hit to economic growth from tariffs.

Bank of America , JPMorgan Chase and Citigroup all fell around 5% each. The yield on the benchmark 10-year Treasury notes was down to a six-month low of 3.938%.

A Labor Department report showed the U.S. economy added far more jobs than expected in March, but Trump's sweeping import tariffs could test the labor market's resilience in the months ahead amid sagging business confidence.

Nonfarm payrolls increased by 228,000 jobs last month, while economists had forecast payrolls advancing by 135,000 jobs.

"With the trade war escalating, investors are ignoring lagging economic indicators like unemployment and focusing on the prospects of materially higher inflation," said Ronald Temple, chief market strategist at Lazard.

Focus now shifts to Fed Chair Jerome Powell's speech at 11:25 a.m. ET for clues on the path of interest rates.

Traders continued to anticipate a more accommodative policy from the U.S. central bank, with money market futures pricing in cumulative rate cuts of 110 basis points by the end of this year, compared with about 75 bps a week earlier.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 10.33-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and a 6.57-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted eight new 52-week highs and 127 new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 11 new highs and 665 new lows.

data.title: Mexico Consumer Morale Falls to 2023-Lows
data.description:
Consumer confidence in Mexico fell to 46 points in March 2025, marking the lowest level since October 2023, compared to 46.3 in February.

There was a deterioration in consumers' assessment of the country's current economic situation (42.1 vs. 42.4 in February) and its future prospects (48.6 vs. 49.8).

At the same time, slight decreases were seen in both households' expectations for the future (57.1 vs. 57.9) weakened while their current situation was seen steady (51.4 vs. 51.4).

data.title: Cocoa is down by 5%
data.description: Cocoa decreased 5% to 8776.32 USD/T

data.title: REG - Origin Enterprises - Holding(s) in Company
data.description:
RNS Number : 7776D Origin Enterprises Plc 04 April 2025  

Holding(s) in Company

Dublin, London, 4 April 2025: Origin Enterprises plc ('Origin' or the 'Company') has received notification from The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc of a decrease in its shareholding in the Company. The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc no longer holds a notifiable interest in the ordinary shares of the Company.

ENDS

Enquiries:

Origin Enterprises plc

Emer Moran

Company Secretary

Tel: +353 (0)1 563 4900

Brendan Corcoran

Head of Investors Relations and Sustainability    

Tel: +353 (0)1 563 4900

About Origin Enterprises plc

Origin Enterprises plc champions sustainable land use through technically-led solutions, empowering our customers to enrich their land so it can achieve its true potential. The Group has leading market positions in Ireland, the United Kingdom, Brazil, Poland and Romania, and is listed on the Euronext Growth Dublin market and the AIM market of the London Stock Exchange.

Euronext Growth (Dublin) ticker symbol:

OIZ

AIM ticker symbol

OGN

Website:

www.originenterprises.com

data.title: Peptonic Medical establishes subsidiary in the US with responsibility for direct sales
data.description:
PEPTONIC Medical AB

As part of its growth strategy in the US market, Peptonic Medical AB (publ) ("Peptonic" or the "Company") has established a new wholly-owned subsidiary, Peptonic Medical Inc. This new entity will be responsible for direct sales of the Company's personal care products within the intimate health sector, including both existing offerings and future product launches.

The establishment of Peptonic Medical Inc. is a strategic move aimed at strengthening the Company’s presence in the US market. By creating a dedicated subsidiary with a sole focus on direct sales under Peptonic’s own brand, the Company aims to promote a more clear and effective connection between its Nordic parent company and the US market.

At the same time, Peptonic will continue to operate through its existing US subsidiary, Common Sense Marketing Inc. (CSM), which manages products sold under white label through collaborations with strategic partners.

For more information contact:

Anna Linton, CEO Peptonic Medical AB

Email: anna.linton@peptonicmedical.se

Phone: +46 70-244 92 07

About PEPTONIC medical AB

PEPTONIC medical AB (publ) is a pioneering Swedish medtech company specializing in the development and sale of clinically proven self-care products for women's intimate health. Through its brands VagiVital and Vernivia, the Company offers a broad over-the-counter portfolio of effective and gentle products designed to help women understand, treat, and prevent common medical conditions in the intimate area. Peptonic's growth strategy centers on geographic expansion, particularly in the U.S. and Europe, while continually enhancing its product portfolio through in-house development and strategic acquisitions.

The Company is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, and operates a subsidiary, Common Sense Marketing Inc., in the U.S. Peptonic Medical was founded in 2009 and has been listed on the Spotlight Stock Market since 2014.

https://news.cision.com/peptonic-medical-ab/r/peptonic-medical-establishes-subsidiary-in-the-us-with-responsibility-for-direct-sales%2Cc4131490

data.title: Primo Brands Initiated at Buy by Truist Securities
Vimian Group AB (publ) terminates liquidity guarantee agreement
data.description: Vimian Group AB (publ) (“Vimian”) today announces that the company has terminated the liquidity guarantee agreement with Carnegie Investment Bank. The purpose of the liquidity guarantee agreement has been to promote liquidity in the company's share, reduce volatility and facilitate trading. Vimian assesses that current trading and liquidity in the share is good and that the benefit of the liquidity guarantee is limited. The agreement expires on 30 April, 2025.
